enums.name: LeadSource
enums.description: Channel through which a lead was acquired. Integration-specific — adding a new source requires new integration code.

enums.name: LeadStatus
enums.description: Lead lifecycle states. Fixed by business workflow (LM-BR-04). Status auto-advances on enrollment events (GP-04).

enums.name: ActivityType
enums.description: Types of system events logged in lead activity trail. Each type maps to a code path.

enums.name: TrialStatus
enums.description: Trial booking lifecycle states. Fixed by business workflow.

enums.name: EnrollmentStatus
enums.description: Core enrollment lifecycle. Instant enrollment for MVP (ET-A-02). Future 'PENDING_APPROVAL' would be a code change.

enums.name: ProgramStatus
enums.description: Program lifecycle states. Only ACTIVE programs visible to parents. Publishing requires Zelle config.

enums.name: FeeType
enums.description: Pricing model type. TOTAL = flat fee for program, PER_SESSION = baseFee * totalSessions. Structurally different calculation paths.

enums.name: DiscountType
enums.description: Standard discount calculation types for coupons.

enums.name: CouponScope
enums.description: Which fee component(s) a coupon discount applies to. Maps to the two-part pricing model (program fee + optional registration fee).

enums.name: TriggerEvent
enums.description: System events that trigger automated email sending. 6 fixed triggers with pre-seeded automations. Adding new triggers requires code changes.

enums.name: DeliveryStatus
enums.description: Email delivery lifecycle. Reusable for future SMS/WhatsApp channels (Phase 2).

enums.name: PaymentStatus
enums.description: Payment lifecycle states. Manual tracking by admin (no automated payment gateway).

duplicateEnums.reason: Same event name used in two contexts: ActivityType logs it as a lead activity entry, TriggerEvent uses it to fire an automated email. Different purposes — not a true duplicate. No consolidation needed.
Same concept (trial was booked) represented as a lead state value and an email trigger. Conceptual overlap but different enum types with different value sets. No consolidation needed.
